entry.title: Re: Custom Form with Custom Data Source in SAP Public Cloud (Grow with SAP)
entry.content:
Hello @Gourab_Dey ,

How can the translation be implemented in such cases?

In the SAP standard system, there is a template (XDP) for each language, and the relevant translations (e.g. for captions) are loaded at runtime.

Should the translations be incorporated into the form along with the data (i.e. captions via a binding)?

Or should there be two templates, as in the standard system, e.g. ZMM_PR_OUTPUT_DE and ZMM_PR_OUTPUT_EN, with a selection made in the coding?

Which approach is best practice?

Kind regards

entry.title: Adaptive Job Server Watch Not Sending Email Alerts
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a question regarding the BO servers.

We have configured watches for all BO servers in the CMC Monitoring tab to monitor their running state. The expectation is that whenever any server changes to the Stopped state, an email alert should be triggered to our mailbox so that we can take timely action.

Recently, we also created a watch for the Adaptive Job Server. However, we are not receiving any email alerts when this server stops.

To verify the configuration, we stopped the other BO servers and confirmed that email alerts are triggered successfully in the Development, QA, Test, and Production environments. The issue is occurring only with the Adaptive Job Server across all environments.

Our BO version details are as follows:

  • Product: SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform

  • Version: 4.2 Support Pack 9 Patch 13

  • Build: 14.2.9.4527

Could you please help us understand:

  1. Why are email alerts not being triggered for the Adaptive Job Server?

  2. Is monitoring the Adaptive Job Server using a watch expected to generate alerts when it stops, or is this server excluded from such monitoring by design?

Your assistance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Best practice for multiple Reporting Entities in Statutory Report
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition rollout and I would like to clarify the recommended approach for configuring Reporting Entities for statutory reporting.

The customer has two different legal entities (two different Company Codes), each with its own Tax Identification Number and Tax Office.

Example:

Company Code A
Portuguese legal entity with its own NIF
Tax Office: [Tax Office A]
Company Code B
Different Portuguese legal entity with a different NIF
Tax Office: [Tax Office B]

In the Statutory Reporting configuration, under:

Reporting Entity → Enter Parameters Specific to Reporting Entity

there are parameters such as PT_TA_CAE.

My questions are:

For two different legal entities with different NIFs, is the recommended approach to create two separate Reporting Entities (one per legal entity), or can both Company Codes be assigned to the same Reporting Entity with one Leading Organizational Unit?
If multiple Company Codes are assigned to the same Reporting Entity, how are entity-specific parameters such as PT_TA_OFFICE and Tax Identification Number handled?
Is there any SAP Best Practice or recommendation for localization when multiple legal entities are implemented in the same S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system?

Any guidance or experience from similar implementations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Exchange of Experience: Managing French Professional Interview Forms in SAP SuccessFactors After the
entry.content:
Hello SAP SuccessFactors Community,

I am currently working on the implementation and management of French professional interview forms following the latest regulatory reforms, and I would like to exchange experiences with other organizations using SAP SuccessFactors.

I am particularly interested in understanding how other companies have designed and deployed their forms within the system:

  • What types of forms have you implemented (Professional Interview, Mid-Career Interview, End-of-Career Interview, 8-year review, etc.)?
  • How are your forms structured?
  • What sections and questions are included?
  • Have you introduced specific workflows, validations, or approvals?

I would also be interested in learning more about your assignment strategy:

  • Do you use Assignment Profiles, dynamic rules, employee groups, job classifications, seniority criteria, contract types, or other mechanisms?
  • How do you manage special cases and compliance tracking?

Our goal is to ensure both legal compliance and a smooth user experience, so I am keen to benchmark practices and learn from others who have already gone through this process.

I am open to exchanging ideas, best practices, and lessons learned. Feel free to reply here or contact me directly.

Languages: English, French, and Polish

Thank you in advance for sharing your experience!

entry.title: Re: Issue regarding service entry sheet
entry.content: The quickest resolution is to review your PO structure and make sure it has proper service line items with account assignment. If your scrap/service procurement process currently uses material-type items, you will need to adjust to Lean Service Procurement (service lines) for SES to work in Public Cloud.

entry.title: SAP Business AI Platform: What It Means for SAP BTP & SAP BDC Customers – Live Expert Session
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire 2026, SAP introduced one of its most significant platform announcements in recent years: the SAP Business AI Platform (BAIP). By bringing together SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), and SAP Business Transformation Management (BTM), SAP has outlined a vision for how enterprises can build, govern, and scale AI-driven business processes across their landscapes.

While the announcement generated substantial interest, it has also raised an important question within the SAP community:

What does this actually mean for existing SAP BTP and SAP Business Data Cloud customers?

 

For many architects, developers, platform owners, data professionals, and SAP user group members, the strategic direction is exciting—but the practical implications are less clear.

  • Is BAIP primarily a rebranding exercise or a significant evolution of SAP's platform strategy?
  • How will BTP fit into the new landscape?
  • What role does Business Data Cloud play in enabling AI?
  • Will customers need to change their current investments, architectures, or roadmaps?
  • What does this mean for day-to-day operations and technical teams?

These are exactly the questions the SAP community is asking—and exactly the questions we aim to answer.

Bringing Clarity from the Experts

To help SAP customers navigate this important announcement, I have organized a special live community session featuring leading SAP experts who are directly involved in shaping the future of these technologies.

Live Session

SAP Business AI Platform: What It Means for SAP BTP & SAP BDC Customers

📅 Date: July 29, 2026
🕓 Time: 16:00

What We Will Cover

During this session, we will revisit the strategic vision behind the SAP Business AI Platform and discuss how SAP intends to unify platform, data, and transformation capabilities to make agentic AI a practical reality for customers and partners.

The discussion will focus specifically on what BAIP means for:

  • SAP BTP customers
  • SAP Business Data Cloud customers
  • Enterprise architects
  • Application developers
  • Data and analytics professionals
  • Platform operations teams
  • SAP transformation leaders

Our experts will examine both the strategic and operational dimensions of the announcement:

Strategic Perspective

  • The vision behind SAP Business AI Platform
  • How BAIP reshapes the SAP platform landscape
  • The relationship between BTP, BDC, and BTM
  • Implications for customer investment strategies
  • The role of business AI in SAP's future roadmap

Practitioner Perspective

  • Impacts on platform architecture and governance
  • What developers and architects need to know
  • How data foundations evolve with Business Data Cloud
  • Practical considerations for current SAP BTP and SAP BDC users

Meet the Experts

We are fortunate to welcome an exceptional panel of SAP thought leaders:

  • Steffen Schad – Head of Outbound Product Management, SAP BTP
  • KP Sauer – Senior Director Data Warehousing, SAP
  • Dan Kearnan – Senior Director, SAP BTP

This is a unique opportunity to hear directly from experts who can provide both strategic context and practical guidance on how SAP's latest platform direction affects customers today and in the future.

Join the Conversation

SAP user groups have always played a critical role in helping customers understand major technology transitions. With the introduction of the SAP Business AI Platform, many organizations are evaluating how their existing BTP and Business Data Cloud investments fit into the bigger picture.

My goal with this session is simple: to bring clarity to the community and help SAP professionals understand what SAP BAIP means beyond the headlines.

Whether you are responsible for platform strategy, architecture, development, analytics, or data management, this discussion will provide valuable insights into SAP's evolving AI-first platform vision.

Register Now

🔗 Registration:
https://events.sap.com/baip/en_us/home.html

Watch Live on YouTube

📺 Live Stream:
https://youtube.com/live/6r2MNgbrT4Q

Add to Your Calendar

📅 Calendar Link:
https://community.sap.com/khhcw49343/downloadCalendar?message.id=14436867

The SAP community thrives on open discussions and shared learning. I hope you'll join us on July 29 as we explore one of the most important SAP platform announcements of 2026 and gain direct insight into what it means for BTP and Business Data Cloud customers moving forward. We look forward to seeing you there.

Kind regards

Tania

Global User Groups Organization

entry.title: Re: Reverse 553 movement
entry.content:
Dear @N0050,

Movement type 553 posted through an inspection lot cannot be reversed in standard Public Cloud. Reset Usage Decision resets the usage decision data but does not reverse the stock posting. SAP documented workaround is to repost the quantity into quality inspection stock using the appropriate process and then process the newly generated inspection lot correctly. The original material document remains unchanged

If the scrapping was correct and only the cost center was incorrect the original 553 posting cannot be edited. The cost accounting line item can be corrected, provided it is eligible for reposting, using Repost Line Items Cost Accounting (F5549)

More details below:

  • SAP KBA 3359470 – Reset Usage Decision cannot reverse inspection-lot stock postings
  • SAP Help – Repost Line Items – Cost Accounting (F5549)

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise Webinar Series - two sessions this week on 14th and 16th July
entry.content:
We have Two sessions coming up this week in the Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise webinar series, both directly relevant for anyone making architectural decisions around AI.
 
Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise is a 13-part webinar series designed by architects for architects. Each session unpacks a different dimension of the Autonomous Enterprise: from the Business AI Platform and agent development to data foundations, governance, and the Context System. It is a community learning journey where SAP architects, product leaders, and customers come together to work through the What, why, and How of building for an agentic world. All sessions are complimentary and available on-demand.
 
This week We have two sessions!
 
Session 7 - Monday 14 July | 17:00 CET / 11:00 ET / 08:00 PT An Architect's Guide to the SAP Family of Models With Markus Kohler, Simon Knehr , and Ashok Veilumuthu
SAP is the only company that has trained AI models on the full depth of its institutional knowledge — decades of business process expertise, application code, financial outcomes, and industry best practices. This session explores what the SAP Family of Models is, how business context delivers 3.5x higher prediction quality than generic LLMs, and what it means for architects evaluating AI model strategies. If you are deciding between generic foundation models and purpose-built enterprise models — or trying to explain the difference to your leadership — this is the session.
 
Session 8 - Wednesday 16 July | 14:00 CET / 08:00 ET / 05:00 PT -  The AI North Star Architecture and AI Golden Path: From Reference to Reality
 
Every architect asks: what does the target architecture look like, and how do I get there? This session answers both - covering the AI-Native North Star Architecture (how AI, data, applications, and agents converge as a unified system), the AI Golden Path (a guided decision framework from idea to production) and the Architecture Center in General. Includes live demonstrations and worked examples showing how reference patterns translate into real implementation decisions. If you have been looking at the published assets on the SAP Architecture Center and wondering how to apply them, this is where it comes together.
Register once, attend any session: https://events.sap.com/eaa-autent-web/en_us/home.html

You can download and import the updated calendar file to ensure all remaining sessions are added to your calendar.

 Slides and recordings from previous session here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/slides-amp-recordings-from-the-13-part-architecting-the-autonomous/ba-p/14432238#M191674

Feel free to share this with to anyone in your architecture, AI, or technology leadership teams who would benefit.

entry.title: Re: how to create project billing request using API in sa...
entry.content:
Hello @yKim,

Good day!

If you’re on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, use the “Project Billing Request” OData API (service CE_PROJECTBILLINGREQUEST_0001).

Steps:
  1. Create a Communication Arrangement for the API (OAuth 2.0 or Basic auth) and note the service URL.

  2. Create the PBR header via POST to the header entity set (e.g., API_PROJECT_BILLING_REQUEST_SRV A_ProjectBillingRequest) with mandatory fields like CompanyCode, Project, BillingRequestDate, Customer, Currency, and a valid PBR type.

  3. Create items via the items navigation from the header (e.g., …/A_ProjectBillingRequest('<PBR_ID>')/to_ProjectBillingRequestItem).

  4. Submit the PBR by calling the bound “submit” action on the header entity (see Operations on the API page for the exact action name and URL).

  5. Verify with a GET (optionally using $expand to see items).
References:
  • Project Billing Request
  • Project Billing Request data product
Tip: If the submit step fails, check that your PBR type and status are configured correctly and that all mandatory fields (e.g., CompanyCode, Project/WBS, Customer, Currency) exist in your system. If you share the HTTP error and payload, we can help pinpoint the missing piece.

entry.title: Re: Configuring Reason Codes and Action Codes for France B2B Supplier Invoices in SAP
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are currently implementing the eDocument France process (Chorus Pro / PPF / Peppol) in SAP S/4HANA 2023, and we are facing an issue with the following standard process flow:

  1. An invoice is posted in the originating application.
  2. The system automatically creates an eDocument instance in the eDocument Cockpit / "Manage Electronic Documents" app.
  3. We navigate to the eInvoice EU - France process, select the eDocument, and submit it.
  4. The system generates the XML file in the required format and forwards it to the recipient's Access Point.
  5. The recipient's Access Point is expected to forward the eDocument to the final recipient (e.g., tax authority or business partner), and the final recipient should return a response via the Access Points back to the Cockpit/app.

Our issue: Step 5 is not happening for us. The process stops right after the status "Received by Sending Access Point" and never progresses further. We do not receive any confirmation, error, or status update indicating whether the document actually reached the final recipient (PPF/Chorus Pro or the business partner).

Our questions:

  • What exactly happens to the document once it reaches "Received by Sending Access Point"? Is this confirmation coming from our own outbound Access Point, or from the Peppol/PPF network?
  • Is there a follow-up status we should expect (e.g., "Sent to Receiving Access Point", "Delivered", "Accepted/Rejected by Recipient"), and if so, what could cause it to be missing?
  • Are there specific logs, transactions, or monitoring tools (e.g., EDOC_COCKPIT, SLG1, or BTP-side monitoring) we should check to trace where the document is actually stuck?
  • Could this be related to Peppol participant/endpoint configuration (e.g., scheme 0225), or is this more likely an issue on the Access Point provider side?

Any guidance on how to trace this further or what commonly causes documents to remain stuck at this status would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: User not as Member of Role after provisioning IAS to AS ABAP
entry.content:
Hi team,

 

we are currently testing user provisioning to AS ABAP following Docu: SAP Application Server ABAP | SAP Help Portal 

The connection succeeded and we were able to update user Attributes for a testuser.

We are testing the Role assignment with Group "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" wich is representative in CIS and our AS ABAP.

The testuser is assigned to this group in CIS, but wont get assigned in AS ABAP after a read or sync Job. 

The technical user has SAP_ALL so it wont be authorization issues. We can also see that the user provisioning is deleting all users wich were already assigned to that group in AS ABAP when we set parameter "ips.overwrite.existedbefore.assignments =true". So the groups is detected and can be edited.

 

Regarding to target system transformation (default from Docu) the groups are set as following:

{
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.USERLIST[?(@.USERNAME)]"
            }

In the provisioning log we can see that the system is updating the correct role "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" and user "XY"

"group:ZS_SR_X_X_0257,
system=SC2,
content={"ROLE_NAME":"ZS_SR_X_X_0257","USERLIST":[{"USERNAME":"XY"}]}"

Greetings
Rouven

entry.title: Re: SII Spain – Conflict between Operation Date/Fiscal Ye...
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973;

First of all, thank you for your response and for the time you took to explain.

We looked into the date update job option, but ultimately it wasn't a viable solution for our case.

However, after further investigation, we found a configuration point that allows the dates to be aligned in a way that better fits our needs: Define Derivation Rules for Business Transaction Types (SSCUI 105884).

I wanted to share it with the community in case it's useful to others, and also thank you again for the initial guidance.

Best regards, and thanks again.

Álvaro Fernández

entry.title: License Portal Down - Urgent Renewal for AS ABAP 7.52 Dev Edition (NPL)
entry.content:
Hi the SAP Community,

I am currently using the SAP AS ABAP 7.52 Developer Edition (MiniSAP), and my license is going to expire very soon on July 17.

I am trying to renew it, but the official license portal (go.support.sap.com/minisap) is currently down and showing an error message.

 

I already contacted the Customer Interaction Center (CIC), but since I am a free user without S-User authorizations, they told me they could not process a standard support case for me.

Could you please advise on how free users can renew their license keys during this portal downtime, or let us know when it might be back online?

My System ID is NPL - SAP NetWeaver 7.x (Sybase ASE).
(For privacy and security reasons, I have omitted my Hardware Key here, but I can provide it via private message if you are able to generate a key for me).

Thank you so much for your support!

Best regards,
Kunihiro Nakano

entry.title: Automatic Payment Run splits payment into two bank transfers for the same supplier in SAP Public Clo
entry.content:
Dear community:

I'm facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud regarding the Automatic Payment Run.

For one supplier, the payment proposal generates two separate bank transfers, even though we would expect a single payment.

The supplier has several open items that should be paid together. However, during the payment run, one invoice is separated from the rest, resulting in two payment instructions:

  • Payment 1: Three supplier invoices.
  • Payment 2: One supplier invoice.

While reviewing the open items, I noticed that the three invoices included in the first payment have document type KR, whereas the invoice that is paid separately has document type RE.

At first glance, this seems to be the only relevant difference, although I wouldn't expect the document type alone to cause the payment run to split the payment.

Has anyone experienced this behavior in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Are there any standard payment grouping rules, configuration settings, or master data that could cause invoices for the same supplier to be split into separate bank transfers?

Additional information:

  • Same supplier.
  • Same company code.
  • Same payment method.
  • Same bank account.
  • Same payment run.
  • Same posting date/value date.
  • Only one invoice has document type RE, while the others are KR.

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: OAuth token auto retrieval not working in IFLOW
entry.content:
Hi,

CPI does natively support automatic OAuth token fetching — the Groovy workaround should not be necessary in the iFlow. The fact that the error occurs without it points to a configuration issue, not a CPI capability gap.

How Native OAuth Should Work in CPI:
When the HTTP Receiver Adapter is configured correctly, CPI handles the full OAuth lifecycle automatically:

iFlow execution
→ CPI checks token cache
→ If expired/missing: calls token endpoint using stored credentials
→ Injects Bearer token into outbound HTTP request
→ Caches token until expiry
→ Calls Layer7 with valid token
This requires the security material to be configured as OAuth2 Client Credentials (not a plain "User Credentials" artifact), and the Receiver HTTP adapter must reference it via the credential alias.

Possible Causes for "Service Not Available"

Wrong credential type in security material: It must be an OAuth2 Client Credentials, not User Credentials
Token endpoint URL misconfigured: The token URL in the OAuth artifact must be the Layer7 token endpoint, not the resource URL
Grant type mismatch: Layer7 may require client_credentials but artifact may be set differently
Token response format unexpected: CPI expects standard JSON {"access_token": "...", "token_type": "Bearer"} — Layer7 custom response may break parsing
Credential alias mismatch in adapter: The alias in the HTTP Receiver channel must exactly match the artifact name
Proxy/network issue to token endpoint: CPI may reach the resource URL but not the token endpoint if they are on different paths

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Hi all, We are currently implementing the eDocument France process (Chorus Pro / PPF / Peppol) in SAP S/4HANA 2023, and we are facing an issue with the following standard process flow:

  1. An invoice is posted in the originating application.
  2. The system automatically creates an eDocument instance in the eDocument Cockpit / "Manage Electronic Documents" app.
  3. We navigate to the eInvoice EU - France process, select the eDocument, and submit it.
  4. The system generates the XML file in the required format and forwards it to the recipient's Access Point.

  5. The recipient's Access Point is expected to forward the eDocument to the final recipient (e.g., tax authority or business partner), and the final recipient should return a response via the Access Points back to the Cockpit/app. Our issue: Step 5 is not happening for us. The process stops right after the status "Received by Sending Access Point" and never progresses further. We do not receive any confirmation, error, or status update indicating whether the document actually reached the final recipient (PPF/Chorus Pro or the business partner). Our questions:

  6. What exactly happens to the document once it reaches "Received by Sending Access Point"? Is this confirmation coming from our own outbound Access Point, or from the Peppol/PPF network?
  7. Is there a follow-up status we should expect (e.g., "Sent to Receiving Access Point", "Delivered", "Accepted/Rejected by Recipient"), and if so, what could cause it to be missing?
  8. Are there specific logs, transactions, or monitoring tools (e.g., EDOC_COCKPIT, SLG1, or BTP-side monitoring) we should check to trace where the document is actually stuck?
  9. Could this be related to Peppol participant/endpoint configuration (e.g., scheme 0225), or is this more likely an issue on the Access Point provider side? Any guidance on how to trace this further or what commonly causes documents to remain stuck at this status would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Manage Tax Items – Field Reference Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

Manage Tax Items (MTI) is an application in SAP S/4HANA that helps finance teams review, correct, and track tax reporting dates on accounting documents. It is part of the Document Reporting Compliance (DRC) solution and is used for statutory tax reporting purposes.

  • MTI application — the main screen where users can search, filter and correct tax items for a selected reporting period
  • MTI Analytics View — an analytical screen that allows aggregated reporting, tax box groupings and KPI analysis on tax items

This guide explains every field available in the MTI application — what it means, where it appears, and a sample value — so that both business users and developers can quickly understand the data.

Overview of the Two Views

Symbol

What it means

YES

This field exists in this view / screen

YES (present in tax item view also)

This field is visible in both the Document Header View and the Tax Item View of the Manage Tax Items application

NO

This field does not exist in this view / screen

Column guide — refer legend above:

Key Fields

The first five fields below are the core identifiers that uniquely identify each tax item — they are used in both the MTI application and the MTI Analytics view. The Analytics view adds three more fields related to tax box groupings, which means a single tax document line can show up more than once in the analytics screen — once for each tax return box it belongs to.

Field Name

Manage Tax Items App

MTI Analytics

Description

Sample Value

CompanyCode

YES
(present in tax item view also)

YES

Company Code of the accounting document

1010

AccountingDocument

YES
(present in tax item view also)

YES

Accounting Document Number

5100000001

YES
(present in tax item view also)

YES

Fiscal Year (cast to fis_gjahr_no_conv in Cube)

2024

ChangedOnDateTime

YES

YES

Timestamp of last change to tax reporting date. Preview: coalesced to 19700101000000; Cube: cast to srf_changed_on

# / 21.06.2024 15:01:39

TaxItem

YES

YES

Tax item counter within the accounting document

1

TaxBox

NO

YES

Tax return box number from tax item grouping configuration

BOX001

TaxBoxStrucValidityStartDate

NO

YES

Start date of validity for the tax box structure

01.01.2024

UnifiedTaxBoxStructureType

NO

YES

Unified tax box structure type identifier

DE_VAT

 

Document Date Fields

Manage Tax Items App

If TaxReportingDate is empty, then PostingDate = ReportingDate; otherwise TaxReportingDate = ReportingDate

YES

Date on which the tax becomes chargeable, i.e., the point at which the tax authorities acquire the legal right to claim payment

Document Identification Fields

Description

The document type classifies the accounting documents. It is stored in the document header. (e.g. KR = vendor invoice, SA = G/L posting)

Tax Code & Classification Fields

Amount Fields

The MTI application shows tax amounts in the reporting currency only (e.g. EUR). The MTI Analytics view additionally shows amounts in the company local currency, and also includes a pre-calculated total amount (tax base + tax) so you do not need to add them up manually.

(decimal separator depends on user settings)

(decimal separator depends on user settings)

NO

11900.00
(decimal separator depends on user settings)

ReportingCurrency

(present in tax item view also)

Currency key for all reporting currency amounts

EUR

Business Partner Fields

Field Name

Manage Tax Items App

MTI Analytics

Description

Sample Value

Business partner number (customer or vendor)

BP-000123

Name of the business partner

-

-

CustomerSupplierVATRegn

Address Fields

Field Name

Manage Tax Items App

MTI Analytics

Description

Sample Value

AddressID

YES

NO

Address ID used to join I_Address_2 for business partner address

ADDR-00001

OrganizationName1

YES

YES

Organization name line 1 from the address

Acme GmbH

OrganizationName2

YES

YES

Organization name line 2 from the address

-

OrganizationName3

YES

YES

Organization name line 3 from the address

-

OrganizationName4

YES

YES

Organization name line 4 from the address

-

StreetName

YES

YES

Street name of the business partner address

Hauptstrasse

HouseNumber

YES

42

YES

10115

CityName

YES

City of the business partner address

Berlin

Country key of the business partner address

DE

BPTargetAddressText

NO

Full formatted address text – concatenated from HouseNumber, StreetName, CityName, Country, PostalCode

Reporting & Status Fields

Field Name

Manage Tax Items App

MTI Analytics

Description

Sample Value

ReportingCountry

YES
(present in tax item view also)

YES

Country for which the tax item is reported (cast to glo_country in Cube)

DE

BusinessPlace

YES
(present in tax item view also)

YES

Business place used in certain country localizations

BP01

TaxItemIsIncluded

YES

YES

I = included from a different tax reporting period; E = excluded from that respective reporting period; # = the item is in its original reporting period (no date change was made — this is the normal case)

I, E, #

StatryRptRunStatus

YES

NO

Statutory reporting run status. Displayed as a radio button (checkbox indicator) in the MTI Preview UI. SOK = document successfully reported in a statutory run; empty = not yet reported

SOK

TaxItemIsReported

NO

YES

Boolean indicator: tax item has already been reported in a statutory run

X

TaxRptgDateIsActive

YES

NO

Indicator: tax reporting date feature is active for the company code. Not visible in MTI Preview — used internally to control field editability

X

LastChangedByUser

YES

YES

User ID who last changed the tax reporting date

JSMITH

TotalNumberOfComments

YES

NO

Total count of comments on the tax item date change log

3

Comments

YES

NO

Latest comment text from the tax reporting date change log

Date corrected to March

DeclarationInformationText

NO

YES

Documents accepted by government (report run status = SOK). Provides information on which entity, category and date the document was declared. Format: Entity:Category:Date (YYYY-MM-DD)

VAT:A:2024-03-31

 

Summary

The Manage Tax Items application has two main views — the List Page and the Analytics View. The List Page is used day-to-day by finance users to review and correct tax reporting dates. The Analytics View provides deeper insight through aggregated reporting and tax box groupings. Understanding which fields appear in which view helps both business users and developers quickly find the right data — and avoid confusion when a field they expect is not visible on screen.

 

Technical Details

MTI Analytics View (C_ManageTaxItemQuery): Analytical query projection built on top of I_ManageTaxItemCube. Used for embedded analytics, KPI tiles and reporting. Exposes all analytical fields including tax box groupings, company code currency amounts and calculated gross totals.

 Manage Tax Items List Page View (C_TaxDocumentStRpActivity): The CDS view that powers the Manage Tax Items Fiori application list page. It provides operational fields used for reviewing, filtering and correcting tax reporting dates on accounting documents.

 


Tips & Best Practices

Tips for the MTI application

  • Use the MTI List Page when you need to review or correct the Tax Reporting Date on individual accounting documents.
  • The Tax Reporting Date feature must be activated at company code level before it can be used. Check the "Tax Reporting Date Active" field to confirm it is switched on for your company code.
  • Always use the Tax Reporting Date field as the main date for filtering and reporting — not the Reporting Date, which is used internally by the system.
  • The Comments field shows the latest note added by a user when changing a tax reporting date. The Total Number of Comments field shows how many notes have been added in total for that document.

Tips for the MTI Analytics View

  • Always open the MTI Analytics View with the required filters — Company Code and Reporting Country are mandatory to ensure correct results.
  • When using Tax Box groupings in reports, note that one document line can appear in multiple rows — once per tax box it belongs to. Group your data by Tax Box to avoid counting amounts more than once.
  • Use the Total Gross Amount fields for KPIs and dashboards — they are pre-calculated by adding the Tax Base Amount and Tax Amount together, so you do not need to calculate them manually.
  • Use the Tax Item Included indicator to understand the status of each item: # means the item belongs to the selected period normally, I means it was moved into this period from another, and E means it was excluded from this period.
  • The Tax Item Reported field tells you whether a document has already been submitted to the tax authority. Use this to separate already-reported items from those still pending.
  • For EU One Stop Shop (OSS/IOSS) reporting, use the One Stop Shop Scheme field to filter out cross-border B2C transactions from your regular domestic tax items.

 

What's Next

This blog covers the core fields available in the current release. As SAP S/4HANA continues to evolve, new fields will be added to these views — particularly around extended tax number support, additional statutory reporting statuses, and further OSS/IOSS reporting enhancements. This document will be updated accordingly when new field additions are delivered.

 

 

Note: This page will be updated as new fields are added in future releases. For field additions or corrections, please raise a request to the MTI development team.

Tags: Manage Tax Items | MTI | S/4HANA | Tax Reporting | CDS Views | Finance | Fiori | Analytics

entry.title: Simplified and Flexible SAP Concur Integration for Service Order Expense Posting
entry.content:
Introduction  

SAP Concur integration enables you to post travel-related expenses directly against service order items. The Concur Relevant indicator at the service order item level controls which service order items are transferred to SAP Concur—only items marked as relevant and in Released status are eligible for export as cost objects for expense postings. 

For organisations where service orders are predominantly generated from solution orders, expense items (fixed price or non-billable) arrive already in released status. The Concur relevance field locks immediately, blocking any manual intervention. Combined with the inability to enable phased rollouts, set intelligent defaults, or control integration at a granular level, these limitations demanded a fundamental rethink. 

SSCUI-based configuration transforms Concur integration from manual selection to intelligent, rule-driven automation. By defining rules based on transaction type, sales organization, product, and item category, the system automatically determines Concur relevance at creation time—before release status locks the field. This enables controlled adoption across divisions, automatic flagging based on business logic, and seamless integration for solution-order-driven workflows. 

This guide covers the complete implementation journey: SSCUI configuration strategies, end-to-end UI workflows from solution order creation through expense reporting and FI posting, and technical architecture.

 

SSCUI Configuration Guide 

SSCUI (Self-Service Configuration UI) is your control center for automating Concur integration. Instead of manually checking boxes on every service order, you set up rules once, and the system automatically handles Concur flagging based on your business logic. 

 

How SSCUI Works: Decision Flow Overview   

When you open SSCUI configuration, you'll see a table with these below columns. 

The diagram above illustrates how SSCUI-based Concur integration determines whether service order expense items should be processed through Concur 

When a service order item is created, the system follows this logic: 

  1. SSCUI Evaluation: System queries the configuration table using the Service Order attributes 
  2. Concur Relevance Check:
    • If Concur Relevance = YES: 
      • System checks the "Is Default Flag" 
        • If Default = YES: Concur Relevance checkbox on UI is automatically checked 
        • If Default = NO: Concur Relevance checkbox on UI remains unchecked 
      • Field editability is determined by "Is Editable" flag 
    • If Concur Relevance = NO: 
      • Concur Relevant field is disabled (greyed out)   
      • Item is not eligible for Concur processing 
  3. Order Save: Service order is saved with the determined Concur relevance status  

Understanding the SSCUI Configuration Screen 

When you open SSCUI configuration, you'll see a table with these below columns. 

Think of SSCUI configuration as creating "IF-THEN" rules: 

IF an item matches these characteristics → THEN apply these Concur settings 

Every rule you create has two parts: 

Part 1: Matching Criteria (The "IF" Part) 

These fields define which items the rule applies to: 

Field 

What It Means 

Example 

Transaction Type 

Type of service order 

SVO1 (T&M), SVO2 (Fixed Price) 

Sales Organization 

The sales org assigned to the order 

1010, 1070, etc. 

Distribution Channel 

Channel through which the service is delivered 

10 (Direct), 20 (Partner) 

Division 

Business division within the organization 

01 (Field Service), 02 (Projects) 

Product ID 

The specific product or service being ordered 

SRV_02 

Material Group 

Product grouping for categorization 

SERVICES, MAINTENANCE 

Item Category 

Type of expense item  

SRVE (Fixed Price Expense), non-billable 

 

Special Character: * (Asterisk) = "Any" 

  • Sales Org = * means "applies to all sales orgs" 
  • Product = * means "applies to all products" 

Part 2: Control Flags (The "THEN" Part) 

These fields define what happens when an item matches:  

Flag 

Usage 

Options 

Concur Relevance 

Determines whether items matching the configuration criteria are eligible for Concur integration. 

YES = Enable / NO = Disable 

Default Flag 

Determines whether a service order item should be marked as Concur-relevant by default at the time of service order creation. 

YES = Auto-check / NO = Leave unchecked 

Is Editable 

This option controls whether users can manually override the system's determination 

YES = Users can edit / NO = Locked 

Accessing SSCUI Configuration 

User Actions: 

  1. Login to the system using the user BPC_EXPERT  
  2. Open the App Manage Your Solution 
  3. Click on Configure Your Solution 
  4. Provide Application area Service and Sub Application Area Integration 
  5. Find the SSCUI named Integration with SAP Concur (SSCUI ID 107355) 
  6. Click on the line to open the item detail page 
  7. Click on Configure under Configuration Steps  
  8. Click on New Entries button 
  9. Use the value help(F4) to select valid values for each field 
  10. Select values for all seven matching criteria fields 
  11. Use specific values for targeted rules, or `*` for broader rules 
  12. Set Concur Relevance Flag = YES or NO 
  13. Set Default Flag = YES or NO   
  14. Set Is Editable Flag= YES or NO 
  15. Click on Save 

     

Understanding Configuration Priority: Which Rule Wins? 

When you configure multiple rules in SSCUI, you might have situations where a service order item matches more than one rule. So which rule does the system apply? The answer lies in rule specificity—the more specific the rule, the higher its priority. 

  • How the System Determines Priority : The configuration columns have weightage from left to right—the leftmost columns carry the highest weight, and the weight decreases as you move right. When evaluating rules, the system prefers the rule with the most specific matches (fewest * entries). 
  • Column Weightage (High to Low): Transaction Type → Sales Org → Distribution Channel → Division → Product ID → Material Group → Item Category 
  • Real-World Example: Two Matching Rules 

         You have two configuration entries

  • Rule 1 (More Specific): Type: SVO1 | Sales Org: 1010 | Division: 10| Channel: 00 → Concur Relevance: YES | Default Flag : YES | Is Editable: YES  
  • Rule 2 (Less Specific): Type: SVO1 | Sales Org: 1010 | Division: * | Channel: * → Concur Relevance: YES | Default Flag : YES | Is Editable: NO  
  • Service order created with: Type SVO1, Sales Org 1010, Division 10, Channel 00 
  • Result: System applies Rule 1 because it has specific values for Division and Channel (not *). The item will be Concur relevant, auto-checked, and editable (following Rule 1's setting). 

 

End-to-End Process Flow in the UI 

This section walks you through the complete end-to-end process in the user interface, starting from Solution Order creation through Service Order processing with automatic SAP Concur integration.

The same configuration also works seamlessly when the process starts with a Service Quotation or a Service Order.

Step 1: Create Solution Order with Service Items 

The journey begins with creating a solution order that will generate service orders with expense items. 

User Actions: 

  1. Navigate to the Manage Solution Orders application   
  2. Click "Create" to start a new solution order.  
  3. Enter the below required header informations:
    • Sold-to Party 
    • Sales Organization 
    • Distribution Channel 
    • Division 
    • Description

       

  4. Add the expense items and change the item category as fixed price
  5. Click on Release All items
  6. Click on Save 
  7. Click on Refresh 
  8. Go to the Transaction History and check Service order created
  9. From the Transaction navigate to the app Manage Service Orders

     

  10. Click on Edit 
  11. Check the expense item and ‘Concur Relevant’ flag is set by default Note : To display the Concur Relevant column in the item table, choose the Personalise ( ) icon. On the Columns tab, select the Display checkbox for Concur Relevant. Choose Save to apply your changes.

Step 2:  Service Order Integration with Concur 

For more detailed information, please refer to the Help documentation, specifically  Service Order Integration with Concur section.

User Actions: 

  1. Login to the S/4 HANA system using user CONF_EXPERT_BUS_NET_IN
  2. Open the App ‘Concur Integration Setup’ 
  3. Check the communication Arrangement  
  4. Select the required system line  

     

  5. Click on Other cost objects -> Service order Item-> Manual Export 
  6. Enter the Service Document ID in the Service Order Header. Then, under System, clear (untick) the Test Mode checkbox. 

     

  7. Click on Execute 
  8. Check the overview and find the service order item is successfully replicated to the concur system 

     

Step 3 : App ‘Concur Communication Monitor’ 

User Actions:

  1. Open the App ‘Concur Communication Monitor’ 
  2. Click on number of ‘successful objects’ in the line CostObjects/Service order items 
  3. Check the Service Document number which is successfully replicated in the concur system 

Step 4:  Login to the concur system 

User Actions: 

  1. Click on create or Create Expense Report 
  2. Fill the below details in the create New Report 

    Report Name 

    Report Start Date 

    Type : Select the (SI) Service Order Item 

    Cost Object ID : Select the successfully replicated Service Order ID-Item No- type 

     

  3. Click on Create Report 
  4. Check Report created  
  5. Manually add expense in the report by clicking ‘Add Expense’
  6. Select the type of Expense
  7. Provide the below details :

    Transaction Date , Amount, City of purchase 

  8. Click on Save Expense 
  9. Click on Submit Report 
  10. Again click on Submit Report in the pop up  
  11. Click on Accept & Continue 
  12. Check Report Submitted 
  13. Check the created Report Number 

Step 5: Go back to the S/4 HANA system 

After creating the report in the Concur system, check the Report Number in S/4 HANA system 

User Actions: 

  1. Open the application Concur Posting Documents Monitor 
  2. Click on Feedback Loop->Step-by-Step->Import Documents from the Queue 
  3. Select All documents and click on Enter

     

  4. Check the previously created Report Number is present in this application and Document status is New 
  5. Click on Feedback Loop->Step-by-Step->Send Acknowledgement 
  6. Verify the Acknowledgement checkbox is Ticked means it is acknowledged 
  7. Click on Feedback Loop->Step-by-Step->Post 
  8. Check the posting message below 
  9. Verify the document status ‘Successful’ 
  10. Click on the Document ID 
  11. Check the selected posting document and click on cancel   
  12. Click on Feedback Loop->Step-by-Step->Send Confirmation 
  13. Check the below message after posting confirmation 
  14. Verify the confirmation Sent Checkbox is Ticked 

     

Step 6: Go back to the concur System

User Actions: 

  1. Open the previously posted Report and check the report status as ‘Processing Payment’ 

     

  2. Now, Refresh it and verify the report status ‘Sent for Payment’. So after posting confirmation, the report status became ‘Sent for Payment’ 

     

  3. Click on Audit Trail 

     

  4. Check the Document number which posted successfully  

Note : Service order items marked as Concur Relevant are excluded during  service confirmation creation, so you cannot create a service confirmation for these items.

entry.title: Re: BRF+ Output Management – Repeat Print/Reprint Functio...
entry.content:
Try the below approach

Go to the relevant Settlement Management Fiori app (e.g., Manage Settlement Documents). Open the settlement document. Navigate to the Output Control tab. Here you see all outputs triggered via BRF+. You can repeat, reprint, or resend the output directly from this tab.
Alternatively, use the Output Management – Reprocessing Fiori app (standard app ID: F1597). This app allows you to search for documents with failed or pending outputs and reprocess them. It works across applications that are BRF+ enabled, including Settlement Management.

entry.title: Re: standad api in sap public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @abdelrahman_khaimse5f2c1,

SAP provides standard released APIs for this requirement in Public Cloud. The primary API is Material Stock – Read. It enables an external system to retrieve current stock information by material, plant, storage location, inventory stock type, base unit of measure, and stock quantity

More details below:

SAP Help Material Stock – Read

The relevant entity is A_MatlStkInAcctMod. SAP documented example selects fields such as:

  • Material
  • Plant
  • StorageLocation
  • InventoryStockType
  • MaterialBaseUnit
  • MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit

A typical request is based on:

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV/A_MatlStkInAcctMod

More details below:

SAP Help Example: Get Stock for a Material and Stock Type at Storage-Location Level

If the third party only requires materials for which a stock record exists the Material Stock – Read API should be sufficient. The results can be filtered by material, plant, storage location, and stock type using standard OData query options. If the third party must also receive all material, plant, storage location assignments, including materials with zero stock the stock API should be combined with the standard Product API – Version 2. This API provides product master data through OData V4 and includes plant- and storage-location-related entities

More details below:

SAP Help Product – Version 2

The ProdPlantStorageLocation entity can be used to retrieve the product, plant and storage-location assignments. The results can then be combined with the stock records using:

Product/Material + Plant + StorageLocation

More details below:

SAP Help Product Plant Storage Location

If the third party also requires plant master information, such as plant names or configuration details the standard Plant – Read API can be used. Its service name is API_Plant_2

More details below:

SAP Help Plant – Read

Therefore the recommended integration design is:

  1. Use Product – Version 2 to retrieve all relevant material, plant and storage-location assignments when zero-stock combinations must also be included
  2. Use Material Stock – Read to retrieve the current stock quantities
  3. Join the results using material, plant and storage location
  4. Aggregate the storage location quantities when the third party requires stock totals at plant level

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Exclusive Hands-On Workshop for SAP CX Customers: Extend SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Are you an SAP Sales Cloud V2 or SAP Service Cloud V2 customer looking to accelerate innovation while maintaining a clean core? Join our exclusive hands-on workshop designed specifically for SAP CX customers to explore how SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) enables you to build extensions, automate business processes, and leverage AI-driven capabilities.

This Bring Your Own Tenant (BYOT) workshop provides practical, hands-on experience with real-world scenarios that demonstrate how SAP BTP can help organizations extend and enhance SAP Customer Experience solutions without modifying the core application.

What You Will Learn

During this interactive workshop, you will learn how to:

  • Build side-by-side extensions using SAP Build Code
  • Develop custom business logic and KPI microservices
  • Create and deploy UI and web applications on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
  • Automate external approval workflows and lead creation using SAP Build Process Automation
  • Explore Joule Skills and AI Agents to enhance sales and service operations
  • Apply SAP BTP best practices for clean-core extensibility and intelligent automation

Who Should Attend?

This workshop is exclusively intended for SAP Customer Experience (SAP CX) customers, including organizations using:

  • SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2

It is ideal for Solution Architects, Technical Consultants, Developers, Application Owners, and Innovation Teams who are looking to extend and automate their SAP CX landscape using SAP BTP.

Workshop Details

Date 23 July 2026

WorkshopExtending SAP CX with SAP BTP: Build, Automate & Innovate
FormatBring Your Own Tenant (BYOT) Hands-on Workshop
AudienceExclusive for SAP Sales Cloud V2 and SAP Service Cloud V2 Customers
Registerhttps://events.sap.com/apj-cx/en_us/home.html

Why Attend?

Discover how SAP BTP empowers SAP CX customers to:

  • Extend SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 while preserving a clean core
  • Build custom applications and business services
  • Automate business processes using low-code/no-code tools
  • Incorporate AI-powered capabilities with Joule Skills and AI Agents
  • Accelerate digital innovation with SAP Build and SAP BTP

If your organization is looking to modernize and innovate its SAP Customer Experience landscape, this workshop offers the practical knowledge and hands-on experience needed to get started.

Seats are intended exclusively for SAP CX customers, ensuring focused discussions, relevant use cases, and an engaging learning experience tailored to your business needs.

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Hi all, We are currently implementing the eDocument France process (Chorus Pro / PPF / Peppol) in SAP S/4HANA 2023, and we are facing an issue with the following standard process flow:

  1. An invoice is posted in the originating application.
  2. The system automatically creates an eDocument instance in the eDocument Cockpit / "Manage Electronic Documents" app.
  3. We navigate to the eInvoice EU - France process, select the eDocument, and submit it.
  4. The system generates the XML file in the required format and forwards it to the recipient's Access Point.

  5. The recipient's Access Point is expected to forward the eDocument to the final recipient (e.g., tax authority or business partner), and the final recipient should return a response via the Access Points back to the Cockpit/app. Our issue: Step 5 is not happening for us. The process stops right after the status "Received by Sending Access Point" and never progresses further. We do not receive any confirmation, error, or status update indicating whether the document actually reached the final recipient (PPF/Chorus Pro or the business partner). Our questions:

  6. What exactly happens to the document once it reaches "Received by Sending Access Point"? Is this confirmation coming from our own outbound Access Point, or from the Peppol/PPF network?
  7. Is there a follow-up status we should expect (e.g., "Sent to Receiving Access Point", "Delivered", "Accepted/Rejected by Recipient"), and if so, what could cause it to be missing?
  8. Are there specific logs, transactions, or monitoring tools (e.g., EDOC_COCKPIT, SLG1, or BTP-side monitoring) we should check to trace where the document is actually stuck?
  9. Could this be related to Peppol participant/endpoint configuration (e.g., scheme 0225), or is this more likely an issue on the Access Point provider side? Any guidance on how to trace this further or what commonly causes documents to remain stuck at this status would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP BTP - CI - CITS Extension - Generate Technical Specifications Directly from the iFlow Designer
entry.content:
Documentation should not take longer than the integration development itself. 

Introducing CITS: A Free Technical Specification Generator for SAP Cloud Integration

Anyone who has worked with SAP Cloud Integration for a while knows the documentation gap.

You spend time building a complex iFlow, validating router conditions, Content Modifiers, scripts, mappings, adapters, credentials, and resources. But then comes the repetitive part: documenting everything.

In real-world projects, this usually means taking screenshots, opening multiple steps manually, listing headers and properties, checking mapping logic, copying adapter details, and trying to keep a Word document aligned with the latest iFlow version.

This process is time-consuming, error-prone, and often becomes outdated as soon as the iFlow changes.

To help with this, I created CITS — Cloud Integration Technical Specification Generator.

I initially built it for my own delivery work: to improve the quality, consistency, professionalism, and technical depth of my documentation, while making it easier to extend or reuse local project templates.

Now I am sharing it with the SAP Community as a 100% free productivity browser extension.

CITS generates technical specifications directly from SAP Cloud Integration iFlows.

What CITS Does

CITS goes beyond simple screenshots. It analyzes the saved design-time model and generates a structured technical documentation baseline.

The generated specification can include:

  • Architecture overview diagram
  • Process summary
  • Sender and receiver/interface details
  • Adapter and security configuration summary
  • Content Modifier details
  • Headers and properties inventory
  • Router and routing condition summary
  • Script and mapping resources
  • Message Mapping field inventory
  • Resource usage inventory
  • Review notes and assumptions

The generated file is a Word-compatible .DOC document, so it can be reviewed, copied, adjusted, and shared according to each project’s documentation process.

Core Capabilities

Architecture Overview

CITS generates a high-level architecture diagram from the iFlow model, helping reviewers quickly understand the trigger or sender, main process, local processes, outbound calls, receiver systems, adapters, and protocols.

Process and Mapping Inventory

CITS extracts relevant technical details from Content Modifiers and Message Mappings, including headers, properties, source fields, target fields, direct mappings, standard functions, and UDF/custom functions.

Resource Usage Check

Large projects often contain old Groovy scripts, XSLT files, schemas, or mappings that nobody wants to delete because nobody is sure whether they are still used.

CITS includes a resource inventory to help identify resources that are used, referenced, or potentially unused.

Adapter and Security Summary

CITS summarizes technical references such as endpoints, queues, topics, credential aliases, certificate aliases, authentication methods, and connectivity details when they are available in the design-time model.

CITS floating button inside the SAP Cloud Integration iFlow designer.

How to Install and Use CITS

Installation is straightforward.

  1. Open Google Chrome.
  2. Go to the Chrome Web Store.
  3. Search for CITS.
  4. Click Add to Chrome and confirm the installation.
  5. Open your SAP Integration Suite tenant.
  6. Go to an integration package and open an iFlow.

Direct link: SAP CITS

When CITS detects an iFlow designer page, the floating action button appears inside the designer.

🔒Security & Transparency Note: Because CITS was just launched and my developer profile is new, Google Chrome's "Enhanced Safe Browsing" might display a temporary “Proceed with caution / Not considered trusted” popup when you click install.

As an Independent Architecture Researcher, I designed CITS with a 100% local processing architecture. The extension does not use any backend servers, does not expose any credential and transmits absolutely zero data outside your browser window. You can safely click "Continue installation". 

Generating Your First Document in 2 clicks

  1. Open the iFlow.
  2. Save the iFlow if you recently changed it.
  3. Click the CITS button.
  4. Refresh the captured model.
  5. Preview the generated technical specification.
  6. Download the Word-compatible .DOC file.
  7. Adjust the document if your company has an official template.

CITS does not require the iFlow to be deployed. It works from the saved design-time model.

Optional project metadata can be added before generating the technical specification.

iFlow Architecture in the Document

The architecture overview is designed to provide a clean technical view for documentation and peer review.

It is not intended to replace the SAP Cloud Integration graphical editor. Its purpose is to make the generated document easier to understand and review.

Real Iflow:

Canvas of the diagram in the document

 

Generated .DOC technical specification with an embedded iFlow architecture canvas.

Message Mapping Documentation

For Message Mapping scenarios, CITS can document the mapping structure in a searchable format.

The generated specification can include:

  • Source resource
  • Target resource
  • Source fields
  • Target fields
  • Direct mappings
  • Standard functions
  • UDF/custom functions
  • Mapping inventory table

This helps reduce the need to open the Message Mapping editor just to understand basic field-to-field transformations.

Message Mapping documentation with source fields, transformations, target fields, and UDF/standard function visibility.

Template Approach

CITS currently generates one standard technical specification template.

This is intentional.

Every company has its own documentation governance, approval sections, naming conventions, branding, and compliance wording. CITS is designed to generate a strong technical baseline, not to replace internal governance.

Recommended usage:

  1. Generate the CITS technical specification.
  2. Review the technical content.
  3. Copy relevant sections into the official company template if needed.
  4. Add project-specific approvals, governance notes, or business context.

Privacy and Security

CITS runs in the browser.

There is no external backend used to generate the document. The generated .DOC file is created locally from the iFlow information available in the browser session.

As always, companies should review browser extension usage according to their own internal security and compliance policies.

Limitations

CITS is a productivity tool, not an official SAP product.

A few important points:

  • Unsaved iFlow changes may not be reflected.
  • The iFlow should be saved before generating the document.
  • The generated specification should still be reviewed by an integration developer or architect.
  • Some adapter details depend on what SAP Cloud Integration exposes in the design-time model.
  • Company-specific governance sections may need to be added manually.

Final Thoughts

CITS was created to reduce repetitive technical documentation work for SAP Cloud Integration projects.

It does not replace solution design, functional specification, testing evidence, governance, or peer review. It helps generate a consistent technical baseline faster.

The objective is simple:

Less manual documentation effort. More consistent technical review.

I hope this helps SAP Integration developers, consultants, architects, and project teams dealing with recurring documentation requirements.

Feedback is welcome.

Related Independent Solo Research Deliverables and SAP SCN Community Contributions

For readers interested in the broader research path behind CITS, these are some of the topics I have been publishing about since January 2026:

To be completely honest, I am well aware that dense topics focused on deep architecture and real governance typically attract a low level of immediate digital engagement. The reason is simple: the content I publish requires a foundational level of structural abstraction and engineering mindset that goes far beyond the operational, "click-and-configure" routine most of the market is used to consuming.

However, vanity metrics do not dictate the intrinsic value of what is engineered. If any of the topics listed below has helped you view the integration ecosystem through a more mature lens—even if invisibly, without a single "like" or public reaction—then my objective has been completely achieved.

The year 2026 has been a period of intensive, solo, and entirely independent technical production. My sole motivation remains to deliver both intellectual frameworks and practical tools to elevate the technical baseline of integration developers within the SAP SCN Community, helping anyone who has the drive to break out of the comfort zone.

Beyond SAP CPI "Expert label" - Motivational journey

  • SAP BTP - IS - How to become a SAP Integration Suite Consultant beyond SAP CPI - SAP APIM Part I
  • SAP BTP - IS - How to become a SAP Integration Suite Consultant beyond SAP CPI - SAP EM/AEM Part II
  • SAP BTP - IS - How to become a SAP Integration Suite Consultant beyond SAP CPI - SAP IA - Part III
  • SAP BTP - IS - How to become a SAP Integration Suite Consultant - Part IV

  • SAP CPI - Smart JMS Retry with Exception-Based Routing in SAP Integration Suite
  • SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Event Routing per Domain with Metadata-Driven Consumer Orchestration
  • SAP BTP - Integration Suite: Clean Architecture vs The Frankenstein Landscape

EventSmartKafka - Custom Kafka Adapter full open source - License Apache 2.0

Step by step how to create a custom adapter using SAP ADK for SAP Cloud Integration, in this case the adapter is for KAFKA integration

  • SAP BTP - CI - Building Open-Source Custom Adapter EventSmartKafka from Zero with SAP ADK - Part II

  • SAP BTP - CI - EventSmartKafka - Custom Kafka Adapter From .ESA v1.1.0 - Validated Runtime - Part IV

Github: EventSmartKafka - Custom Resources full open code and .esa for download

Site: www.domain-intent.com

entry.title: Re: Intercompany STO - Use Sales Price (ESP2) as Purchase...
entry.content:
Below are the answers to your Questions

1. Can intercompany purchase price be determined directly from ESP2?
No, not in Public Cloud. ESP2 is a sales-side condition used in SD pricing. On the purchasing side (MM), the system expects PPR0 (price from Purchasing Info Records). Public Cloud restricts cross-framework condition usage; you cannot directly map ESP2 into MM pricing.

2. Is maintaining Purchasing Info Records the standard approach?
Yes, SAP standard requires Info Records for intercompany STO pricing. The receiving company code uses a PO with condition PPR0, which reads from the Purchasing Info Record maintained for the supplying plant/vendor. This ensures consistency and auditability between FI/MM/SD. Alternatively, Use Condition Contracts in Settlement Management or Advanced Intercompany STO (scope item 5HP), but these still rely on MM-side conditions, not SD ESP2.

3. Is the restriction on Access Sequences only in demo systems?
No, it is a global restriction in Public Cloud. In S/4HANA Public Cloud, customizing of access sequences, condition tables, and pricing procedure frameworks is locked down. You can copy existing condition types (like you did with ZVPV), but you cannot change their access sequences. This limitation applies equally in productive tenants.

4. SAP Best Practice for Intercompany STO Pricing
Use scope item 1P9 (Classic Intercompany STO) or 5HP (Advanced Intercompany STO). Maintain Purchasing Info Records for the supplying plant/vendor. Price determination flows via PPR0 in MM, not ESP2. If you want alignment with sales-side ESP2, you must ensure synchronization of condition records between SD and MM (manual or via integration tools), but not direct reuse.

entry.title: Workflow Requirement within ICMR Period close
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to enable workflow to 2 set of approvers during closure of the Reconciliation period within ICMR.

1. The workflow should be triggered to first set of approvers in case the difference exist between company and trading partner balance, but the difference is within tolerance.

2.The workflow should be triggered to second set of approvers in case the difference between company and trading partner balance, exceed the tolerance limit set for the reconciliation case.

While trying to configure this, SAP has provided to select only one of the available option against a matching method- 1. No workflow, 2. Workflow when differences exist, 3. Workflow when difference exceed the set tolerance limit. We have selected option 2 as SAP doesn't allow to select both option 2 and 3 for a single matching method.

We have also tried to create a step within a single workflow to trigger it to second set of approvers in case differences exceed set tolerance limit. However, there is no precondition or amount limit that can be set in the manage workflow app.

Kindly, suggest if there are any existing solution which can be used to achieve this. We want to go for standard SAP solution if possible.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: PR Workflow Issue
entry.content:
We are planning to revise the existing workflow and implement the new approval process based on Document Type.

However, during the configuration of the Purchase Requisition (PR) Flexible Workflow, we observed that the Document Type field is not available in the workflow item-level start conditions in our system.

As the new approval matrix is proposed to be driven based on Document Type, we kindly request your support in verifying whether this field can be enabled or made available in our server for PR workflow configuration.

entry.title: Header-Level Cost Distribution for Partial Goods Receipt
entry.content:
We need to open a raise ticket with SAP before the end of the day regarding a spare parts purchasing scenario.Business Scenario:

  • A Purchase Order (PO) was created for 100 spare parts.
  • Only 80 items have been delivered and will be received through a partial Goods Receipt (GR).

Requirement:
We need to distribute the header-level costs (such as planned delivery costs or other header charges) only across the 80 received items, rather than allocating the costs to all 100 PO items.Could you please advise whether there is a standard SAP solution to achieve this at the header level, without manually distributing the costs at the item level

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

Thank you for the document.

Which of the two we are supposed to follow in the configuration in the Help Portal?

Thanks you in advance.

entry.title: Turn SAP APIs into MCP Tools for AI Agents using SAP Integration Suite Free Trial
entry.content:
The MCP Capability for SAP Integration Suite is finally here, and everyone can try it now!

This last week I've been playing around with the new MCP Server artifact in SAP Integration Suite and created an end-to-end use case that I think is worth sharing with the community.

The idea is simple: take an official SAP API from the Business Accelerator Hub (in this example we will use the Business Partner (A2X) API for S/4HANA Public Cloud, as it has a public sandbox that we can use without the need to have a S/4HANA system), expose it through Integration Suite as an MCP server, and connect it to any MCP-compatible AI client so you can query business data using natural language.  All of this on a free trial account (you can get one here).

Before we dive deep into development I recommend checking this blog to get more details on what is a MCP Server, MCP Tool, and MCP Gateway and how they will work together in SAP Integration Suite to become the endorsed pathway for SAP API access: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/mcp-gateway-in-sap-integration-suite-your-apis-ready-for-the-age-of-agents/ba-p/14438250

What you need before we start:

  • A BTP trial account (free at https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/trial.html)
  • Create a subaccount (the MCP capability is available in both USA and Singapore regions)
  • Create an instance of SAP Integration Suite with the trial plan
  • Activate the Cloud Integration and API Management capabilities in SAP IS (and assign the required roles to your user in the BTP Cockpit)
  • Enable the new Integration Cell runtime (go to Settings -> Runtimes)
  • A SAP Process Integration Runtime service instance with plan integration-flow created in your Cloud Foundry space, with a service key. This gives you the OAuth credentials (clientid, clientsecret, tokenurl) needed to call the MCP server endpoint.
  • Any MCP-compatible AI client (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Continue, or any agent framework supporting MCP)
  • The Business Partner API spec JSON downloaded from SAP Business Accelerator Hub, plus your API key from there

The architecture


The AI client connects to the MCP Server over streamable HTTP, authenticating with an OAuth Bearer token from SAP BTP.
The MCP Server exposes the Business Partner OData operations as tools that the AI model can discover and call.
When a tool is invoked, the MCP Server forwards the request to the API Artifact, which acts as a proxy to the SAP sandbox.
A Content Modifier policy on the API Artifact injects the Business Accelerator Hub API key before hitting the backend — so the AI client only ever needs the OAuth token, no backend credentials.

Note: The "Trust Upstream MCP Authorization" setting on the API Artifact is what makes the internal call work — without it, the API Artifact will reject the request from the MCP Server as unauthorized.

Step 1: Create the API Artifact

Go to your Package → Add → API.
Select Integration Cell as the Runtime profile
Choose URL or Specification as the method
Select Upload and then:

  • upload the Business Partner JSON spec downloaded from the Business Accelerator Hub
  • set the URL to https://sandbox.api.sap.com
  • service type ODATA
  • base path /business-partner-api

Click on Add and Open in API Designer.

In the Policies tab, add a Content Modifier after the Authorization policy. Under Message Header, add:

  • Action: Create
  • Name: APIKey
  • Source Type: Constant
  • Source Value: <your-hub.sap.com-API-key>

In the Authorization policy, check "Trust Upstream MCP Authorization" — this tells the API artifact not to re-validate auth when the MCP server calls it internally. Set the Scope to ESBMessaging.send.

In Target Endpoint, set the full backend URL: https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER

Deploy.

Step 2: Create the MCP Server

Go back to your Package and click on Add → MCP Server → Source Type: API (from a deployed API artifact on Integration Cell):

  • Pick your BusinessPartner API artifact
  • Give it a Name
  • Set MCP Path to /business-partner

Then on the Tools step select the operations you want — select just the GET /A_BusinessPartner if you want to keep it simple. You will be able to add more tools after.

In the Authorization policy, change the scope to match what the integration-flow service key produces: ESBMessaging.send

Deploy.

Step 3: Get credentials

In BTP Cockpit → Cloud Foundry → your space → Service Instances → find the SAP Process Integration Runtime instance with plan integration-flow → create a Service Key. You'll get a clientid, clientsecret and tokenurl.

Fetch a token:

curl -X POST "<tokenurl>/oauth/token" \
  -u "<clientid>:<clientsecret>" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials"

Step 4: Connect your AI client

You can find you MCP server endpoint in the monitoring area of SAP Integration Suite or in the general information of your MCP Server artifact:

https://<your-tenant>.a.integration.cloud.sap/business-partner

The transport is streamable HTTP (not SSE). Pass the OAuth token as a Bearer header in the Authorization field of your MCP client configuration. The exact way to do this depends on your client — most have an MCP server settings file where you can set the URL, transport type, and custom headers.

Once it's working, you can ask your AI assistant things like "list the top 5 business partners" and it calls the MCP tool, hits the SAP sandbox, and returns relevant mock data.

I hope this use case can be a solid starting point for building MCP servers on SAP Integration Suite. You can use this capability to expose any API, from SAP or non-SAP.
I will be covering more advanced use cases in the next series of this post. Stay tuned!
