entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud: Q2 2026 Innovation Highlights
entry.content:
The latest SAP Sales Cloud release introduces new capabilities that help sales and field teams sell smarter, execute faster, and stay productive wherever they work. AI-powered opportunity insights, automated order creation, smarter visit planning, and offline retail execution help sales and field teams improve productivity, respond to customers faster, and deliver better sales outcomes. This blog will cover a few key feature highlights that are worth a closer look. For a more in-depth showcase on these capabilities and more, we invite you to register for the SAP Sales Cloud release highlight webcast.

Here's what's new in SAP Sales Cloud: 

FIELD SELLING:

Dynamic Visit Planning (DVP) on Mobile

Plan the most efficient day in minutes. SAP Sales Cloud will help field teams build an optimized schedule based on working hours, travel preferences, appointment commitments, account priorities, and expected traffic. Easily pin must-visit accounts and re-optimize the route, helping field teams fit in more customer visits, reduce travel time, and stay focused on selling. 

Offline SAP Retail Execution App

Keep field teams productive, even without an internet connection. Field reps can plan ad hoc visits from Calendar, List, or Map views and complete visits entirely offline with access to accounts, contacts, tasks, surveys, notes, attachments, and sales orders, without requiring a full sync. A new Customer Details view brings together account and contact information in one place, making it easy to view sales data, territories, and timelines, as well as add or update contacts while on the go. Reduce downtime, complete more work in the field, and automatically sync updates when connectivity is restored.

On Device Intelligence: Meeting Recorder and Summarization

Never miss important customer conversations. Record meetings and automatically receive a summary, conversation sentiment, and recommended follow-up actions when the meeting ends, turning every customer's conversation into clear next steps. Spend less time taking notes, follow up faster, and keep every customer interaction moving forward.

Overnight Route Planning

Plan multi-day customer trips with ease. Field reps can now plan customer visits for up to seven consecutive days in a single trip. Simply select the accounts and travel dates, and SAP Sales Cloud creates an optimized schedule based on account priorities, AI recommendations, and the most efficient route. Reduce planning time, visit more customers, and make every trip more productive.

 EMBEDDED AI TOOLS:

AI Email-to-Order from Outlook

Turn customer emails into sales orders in just a few clicks. The system reads incoming order emails, extracts the relevant order information, and prepares a sales order for review before submission. Reduce manual data entry, minimize errors, and process orders with greater accuracy.

Account Plan AI Executive Summary

Get a clear overview of any account plan in seconds. Sales teams can generate an AI-powered executive summary that highlights the most important account details, priorities, and next steps, while maintaining full control to edit, tailor, and refine the content before sharing. Review account plans faster, improve team alignment, and focus on the actions that matter most. 

Opportunity AI Summary

Reps get an instant snapshot of deal status without scrolling through notes and emails. This means faster deal reviews and pipeline check-ins. To use it, go into any opportunity and click the generate summary button to see the AI-generated summary.

Visit Priority AI Summary in DVP

Know why an account is worth visiting before you hit the road. Field reps can now view an AI-generated summary explaining each account's visit priority score directly from the map. Understand what influenced the recommendation, prioritize the right accounts with confidence, and make every customer visit count.

Ready for more? 

We’re very excited to showcase these and more highlights on the SAP Sales Cloud Innovation and Foundation webcast on Tuesday, July 28th. In addition to a deeper dive and solution demonstration of these features, we will also share upcoming roadmap items and answer questions in real-time. To dive deeper into specific use cases or for a personalize showcase drop us a note on SAP.com.

entry.title: SAP RDP - German EPR: Packaging in scope, drop shipment workaround, and system participation duty
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have some questions about the German packaging regulation as well as its execution withSAP Responsible Design and Production

  1. Packaging in scope: Packaging that ends up at consumers is considered by the German EPR. Is RDP looking at the usage field and filters for "H" for household? So household packaging is included even if the packaging is shipped to a German trader instead of a German end consumer?
  2. Drop shipment: Pavel already answered a drop shipment question from me in the past. RDP does not support drop shipment at the moment. He indicated that drop shipment is not always relevant for all scenarios. I would like to know if it makes sense to include drop shipment for German EPR for the following scenario. A German supplier directly ships to a German customer. We receive a statistical goods receipt from that supplier and bill to the customer. RDP is able to process this flow if we map the statistical GR_PO to a DL_SO through a custom CDS View. Does this make sense to you?
  3. System participation duty: We differentiate between packaging participating in the system, packaging not participating in the system, and packaging that is exempted from reporting. We need to report on packaging with and without system participation duty. Does RDP consider both through the report category of Dual Systems Germany?

Best regards,

Jessy

entry.title: How to automate MM period closing using the MMPV Close Period app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
We would like to schedule the MM period close job (MMPV) to run automatically on a monthly basis in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
When creating a recurring job for the MMPV transaction directly, the period parameter is mandatory and remains fixed at the value entered at job creation time, it does not auto-increment for each subsequent monthly run.

Our questions:

  1. Is it possible to schedule the period close so the posting period is automatically determined each month?
  2. Does the "Use current date" flag in the scheduling options allow the system to determine the correct MM posting period automatically?
  3. If automatic determination is not supported, is manual maintenance of the period parameter the standard behavior?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to automate MM period closing using the MMPV Clos...
entry.content:
Yes, automatic period determination per monthly execution is fully supported, but it requires using the Close Period for Product Master app (F4619), not the MMPV Close Periods scheduled directly as a background job.

The Close Period for Product Master app (F4619) is the scheduling interface for this operation.
In the parameters sections, when the "Use current date" flag is enabled the system determines the correct posting period from the server's current date at each execution.
This means no manual update to the period parameter is needed between runs.

Steps on how to set up the scheduling job are available in the following SAP Help Portal page: Close Period for Product Master (F4619)

The user running the app (or the technical user for the background job) must have the PRODMASTER_SPECIALIST role assigned with Write access to the relevant company code.

References

  • SAP Help Portal: Close Period for Product Master (F4619)
  • SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library: Close Period for Product Master

entry.title: ODBC Driver for ABAP Released for macOS on Apple Silicon — Plus Linux Library Improvements
entry.content:
In this post, I will briefly explain how to install and use the driver on macOS.

Download the SAPCAR executable

To extract the driver archives, you first need the SAPCAR tool.
Search for sapcar in the SAP Software Center. The exact version is not critical, any recent version available for macOS on arm64 should work (e.g. version 7.53 patch level 1200).
Download the .EXE file. Although the file extension may look unusual, this is the correct executable for macOS. For convenience, rename it to sapcar and make it executable:

cd ~/Downloads
mv SAPCAR*EXE sapcar
chmod a+x sapcar

Download SAPCRYPTOLIB and the ODBC Driver for ABAP

Next, download the latest SAPCRYPTOLIB package and the current version of the ODBC Driver for ABAP. At the time of writing, these are:

SAPCRYPTOLIB: patch level 8604
ODBC Driver for ABAP: patch level 31

Create a new directory for the extracted files. The commands below assume that there is only one version of each archive in your Downloads folder:

mkdir ~/ODBC_driver_for_ABAP
cd ~/ODBC_driver_for_ABAP
~/Downloads/sapcar -xf ~/Downloads/SAPCRYPTOLIB*SAR
~/Downloads/sapcar -xf ~/Downloads/ODBCforABAP*SAR

Install unixODBC

The ODBC Driver for ABAP works with the unixODBC driver manager, which can be installed with Homebrew:

brew install unixodbc

Configure odbc.ini

unixODBC is configured through the odbc.ini file. This file is typically located under the Homebrew prefix. You can determine the prefix by running:

brew --prefix

Create the odbc.ini file in the etc subdirectory of that path. On Apple Silicon systems, this is usually:

/opt/homebrew/etc/odbc.ini

The following example shows a minimal data source configuration for testing:

[MYDSN]
Driver=/Users/<myuser>/ODBC_driver_for_ABAP/ODBC_driver_for_ABAP.dylib
HOST=<hostname>
PORT=443
CLIENT=100
LANGUAGE=EN
SERVICEPATH=/sap/bc/sql/sql1/sap/S_PRIVILEGED
CryptoLibrary=/Users/<myuser>/ODBC_driver_for_ABAP/libsapcrypto.dylib
UidType=alias
TypeMap=semantic
TrustAll=true

Run a smoke test

You can use the iusql tool to test the connection to your ABAP system through the ODBC driver.
In some cases, Homebrew does not configure all required library paths for iusql. If necessary, set the following environment variable before running the test:

export DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/lib

Note that this setting is not persistent. The best way to make it permanent depends on your shell configuration and how you plan to use the driver.
Now connect using:

iusql MYDSN

Once connected, run a simple SQL query:

select * from sys.dummy

You should see output similar to the following:

+------+
| DUMMY|
+------+
| X |
+------+
SQLRowCount returns 0
1 rows fetched

Linux improvement

As an additional improvement, the driver now searches for dependent libraries in its own directory on all platforms. On Linux, this means it is no longer necessary to set LD_LIBRARY_PATH to the driver directory.

entry.title: Re: Enhancing the Standard XML File for Electronic Documents using the BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD
entry.content:
@ankita_bhat this blog, specially being referenced from note 3578879, should be updated or clarified.

In Argentina I had a similar requirement, having an unreleased structure in CS_OUTPUT_DATA parameter: EDO_AR_4291_FECAESOLICITAR_SO (per my understanding, equivalent to EDO_UBL_STANDARD_BUSINESS_DOC2 from the example). Uncommenting this declaration, the error "The use of Table EDO_AR_4291_FECAESOLICITAR_SO1 is not permitted" is received:

METHOD if_edoc_adaptor_cloud~set_output_data.

* DATA: ls_output_fe_local TYPE edo_ar_4291_fecaesolicitar_so1.

Following this blog instructions above:

Proxy Objects must be released for Developer Extensibility by the country
Note: To check if the development objects are released for developer extensibility, select an object, go to Properties and choose API State. If the objects aren't released, create a support case in the relevant component.

I've opened case 816291/2026 in SAP support, but the response was that the object will not be released because it is a proxy structure and hence unstable and subject to change.

So, this left me with the only option to implement the code using field symbols to access and modify each subcomponent of CS_OUTPUT_DATA, as a workaround. Do you have any comments on this?

entry.title: Re: Working with SAP BTP SDK for Android and older Android devices with DigiCert G5 Certificate
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the write-up! Small inconsistency in Step 1: the download link points to the ECC G5 root (DigiCertTLSECCP384RootG5.crt.pem), but the filename and config reference below use the RSA name (digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5) — and SAP Note 3708420 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3708420) links the RSA root instead.

In my testing, every G5-migrated BTP host chained to DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5; I didn't see the ECC root used anywhere. Which is correct to bundle RSA, ECC, or both? And is the ECC link a typo?

Thanks!

entry.title: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
During the fixed bin putaway scenario, if a product has multiple fixed bins assigned, our standard understanding is that the system proposes the bin that comes first based on the sort sequence.

However, our requirement is that when multiple fixed bins are assigned to a particular product, the bin that was added first in the /scwm/binmat should be proposed for putaway, instead of the bin determined by the sort sequence.

Kindly confirm whether this behavior is supported in standard functionality. Also, please let us know if there is any BAdI or enhancement option available to achieve this requirement.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  • Check the sort sequence maintained for the fixed bins in storage type X.
  • In /SCWM/BINMAT, assign multiple fixed bins to the same product for the particular st type.
  • Ensure the fixed bins are created at different times so that each assignment has a different creation timestamp. In a test case, BIN6 was assigned first, followed by BIN2, BIN4, etc.
  • Create the inbound delivery for the product.
  • Create the putaway Warehouse Task (WT) for the inbound delivery.Observe the destination bin proposed by the system:
  • Actual Result: The system proposes BIN2, which has the smallest sort sequence among the assigned fixed bins.
  • Expected Result: The system should propose BIN6, as it was the first fixed bin assigned to the product in /SCWM/BINMAT, regardless of the sort sequence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
Hello,

Standard EWM does not propose a destination bin based on creation timestamp in /SCWM/BINMAT.

If the standard behavior is not enough for your business processes, the following BAdI from Enhancement Spot /SCWM/ES_CORE_PTS can be used to implement custom bin sorting logic:

/SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_FILT_SORT - Filter and Sort Possible Storage Bins
An example implementation is available in the system under class /SCWM/CL_EI_CORE_PTS_FILT_SORT.

Regards,
SAP Technical Support
